source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Skagua [Skagway], Alaska
description: 1897 Gold Rush era photograph taken from the deck of a ship arriving at Skagway, showing prospectors unloading supplies onto a makeshift wharf with cordwood stacked along the shore. One of the earliest photographs of the nascent Skagway settlement.
alt: 1897 photograph from a ship arriving at Skagway showing prospectors unloading supplies onto a makeshift wharf during the Gold Rush
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-12 — downloaded from tile.loc.gov and visually confirmed
